end of world model.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find red dot positions inside standalone shapes
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

# Green shape red dot at row 10, col 28
# Yellow shape red dot at row 28, col 9
print("Green shape red dot:", lines[10][28])
print("Yellow shape red dot:", lines[28][9])

# Also check red crosses
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'r':
        print(f"Red object: id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Try clicking the red dot inside the green standalone shape
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 28}]
result = action(actions)
print(f"\nResult: {result}")

# Check for changes
if last_transition:
    before = last_transition.before_frame
    after = last_transition.after_frame
    before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before.segmentation['nodes']}
    after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after.segmentation['nodes']}
    
    changes = []
    for nid in set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys())):
        bn = before_nodes.get(nid)
        an = after_nodes.get(nid)
        if bn is None or an is None or bn['pixels'] != an['pixels'] or bn['color'] != an['color'] or bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
            changes.append((nid, bn, an))
    
    if changes:
        print(f"\nChanges: {len(changes)} objects changed")
        for nid, bn, an in changes:
            if bn:
                print(f"  id={nid}: {bn['color']}/{bn['pixels']} -> {an['color'] if an else 'REMOVED'}/{an['pixels'] if an else 0}")
            else:
                print(f"  id={nid}: NEW {an['color']}/{an['pixels']}")
    else:
        print("\nNo gameplay object changes - just timer bar")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
